reasoning: Without specific market details, the range of 10 to 12 successful launches represents a narrow band (just 3 integers). Historically, SpaceX's Starship launch rate has been around 3-4 per year (in 2024). Even if they significantly accelerate operational cadence using both pads and rapid reuse, achieving 10 to 12 successful space-reaching launches requires a smooth schedule (roughly 1 launch per month) with no major grounding incidents, yet falling short of a massive ramp-up (13+ launches). The large variance in potential launch numbers (anywhere from 6 to 15+) distributes the probability density broadly. Typical estimates would place the mean expected launches in 2025 between 6 and 10. Consequently, the probability of the actual number of successful flights falling exactly within the 10-12 window is likely bounded between 15% and 30%. A realistic estimate is around 22%.
